assignment.name: calibration
assignment.title: Model AI's effect on one job category
assignment.description: Build a small agent-based simulation (15-20 workers) that models how AI adoption disrupts and reshapes one job category. The most critical early decision is your implementation tool: NetLogo (the standard ABM platform for this course), a general-purpose language (Python with Mesa), or a spreadsheet (Excel or Google Sheets). After choosing the tool, select a job category and define the emergent mechanism. Compare exactly two scenarios: high automation pressure vs. high worker support. Deliver a 1-page memo and one figure (diagram or screenshot).
constraints: Must compare exactly two scenarios: high automation pressure vs. high worker support.
15-20 worker agents, at least 3 discrete states, 1 explicit emergent mechanism.
Must distinguish programmed behavior from emergent outcomes in the interpretation.
AI use must be documented with exact prompts and what was changed manually.

recommended_plan: NetLogo (built-in ABM, visual interface, easy state tracking)
Data entry clerks: emergent mechanism = productivity cliff (workers compete on speed/accuracy against AI, triggering layoffs when threshold crossed)

tasks.title: Set up NetLogo project structure and environment
tasks.deliverable: NetLogo file (.nlogo) with basic world setup, 15-20 worker agents initialized with state variables (skill-level, speed, accuracy, employment-status)

tasks.title: Define worker agent states and transitions
tasks.deliverable: NetLogo code implementing 3 discrete states: employed, retraining, unemployed; with state-change rules and skill/accuracy attributes tracked per tick

tasks.title: Implement productivity cliff emergent mechanism (high automation pressure scenario)
tasks.deliverable: NetLogo code for Scenario A: AI performance metric, worker performance calculation (speed × accuracy), threshold logic triggering layoffs when worker performance < AI baseline, and competitive dynamics visualization

tasks.title: Implement high worker support scenario with retraining mechanics
tasks.deliverable: NetLogo code for Scenario B: alternate rule set where workers below threshold enter retraining state (recover skills over time) instead of layoff; company invests in support programs; track retraining success rate

tasks.title: Add data collection and metrics tracking
tasks.deliverable: NetLogo reporters/monitors for: employment rate, average worker skill level, productivity distribution, layoff count (Scenario A) vs. retraining completion rate (Scenario B); export-to-file capability

tasks.title: Run Scenario A (high automation pressure) simulation
tasks.deliverable: NetLogo simulation output data (CSV or table) from 100+ ticks, screenshot of final state showing employment distribution, productivity cliff visualization

tasks.title: Run Scenario B (high worker support) simulation
tasks.deliverable: NetLogo simulation output data (CSV or table) from 100+ ticks, screenshot of final state showing employment/retraining distribution, comparison to Scenario A

tasks.title: Analyze emergent outcomes and document programmed vs. emergent behavior
tasks.deliverable: Analysis document (1-2 pages working notes) distinguishing: programmed rules (layoff threshold, retraining mechanics) from emergent patterns (distribution of outcomes, systemic inequality, resilience differences)

tasks.title: Generate comparison figure (diagram or dual-scenario screenshot)
tasks.deliverable: High-quality PNG/PDF figure showing side-by-side NetLogo screenshots or line-graph comparison of employment rates, skill distributions, or productivity over time for both scenarios

tasks.title: Write 1-page executive memo
tasks.deliverable: Memo (0.9-1.0 page, single-spaced) summarizing: job category (data entry clerks), emergent mechanism (productivity cliff), key findings from both scenarios, policy implications, explicit callout of AI tool use

tasks.title: Document AI use with exact prompts and manual edits
tasks.deliverable: Appendix or separate text file listing: (a) all prompts given to AI tools (if any used during code generation/analysis), (b) what was accepted vs. manually changed/rewritten, (c) rationale for changes

tasks.title: Quality check and final packaging
tasks.deliverable: Final submission package: memo (PDF), figure (PNG/PDF), NetLogo .nlogo file, AI documentation, README with simulation notes

open_risks: NetLogo learning curve if team unfamiliar; mitigate with 2-3hr tutorial review early (Task 1)
Productivity cliff mechanism may be too sensitive to threshold tuning; requires iterative parameter testing in Tasks 3–4
Scenario B retraining mechanics may not produce visibly different outcomes if success rate too high; may need 2–3 parameter sweeps to calibrate meaningful divergence
Figure clarity: side-by-side screenshots may be hard to compare; line graphs preferred but require additional export/plotting step not in core NetLogo (Task 9 may need external tool)
Memo page limit tight; significant compression of findings required in Task 10
AI documentation requirement (Task 11) retroactive—recommend documenting AI prompts *during* task execution, not after
